name: Aleph Alpha
one_liner: German sovereign AI company developing large language models and the PhariaAI enterprise AI stack for European governments and regulated industries.

ambiguities: The $500M Series B (Nov 2023) headline figure was later reported to include ~€110M in true equity, ~€300M in research grants to subsidiary Aleph Alpha Research, and ~€60M in order commitments — actual equity raised is lower than stated.
Total funding figures vary by source: Tracxn reports $533M, PitchBook $520M; $533M used as best available.

Headcount is uncertain: PitchBook cites 350 employees (2026 profile), press release from April 2026 Cohere merger announcement cites ~200 employees across four German locations; 200 used as more recent press-cited figure.
Valuation of ~€2.7B (~$3B) post Series B reported by theaiworld.org; not officially confirmed.
As of April 2026, Cohere announced acquisition of Aleph Alpha (deal pending regulatory closure); Aleph Alpha remains an independent entity until close.
Founder Jonas Andrulis reportedly stepped down as CEO in late 2025; leadership transition ongoing.
Tech stack inferred from job postings, ZoomInfo, and product documentation — not from an official engineering blog.
